bd.title: Dengue Cases Rise Sharply in Dhaka, Health Officials Urge Increased Precautionary Measures
bd.summary: Health authorities in Dhaka are reporting a significant surge in dengue fever cases as the monsoon season progresses, raising concerns about a potential widespread outbreak. Hospitals are seeing an increase in admissions, prompting the Directorate General of Health Services (DGHS) to issue an urgent appeal for public vigilance and enhanced mosquito control efforts. The government is implementing awareness campaigns and larval eradication drives to curb the escalating situation.

bd.title: Padma Bridge Rail Link Project Faces Delays Amid Land Acquisition, Contractor Issues
bd.summary: The ambitious Padma Bridge Rail Link project, a crucial component of Bangladesh's infrastructure development, is reportedly facing new delays. Challenges related to land acquisition and coordination issues with contractors are being cited as primary reasons for the slowdown. The government remains committed to completing the project, which aims to significantly boost connectivity and economic activity in the southwestern region.

bd.title: Inflation Remains High as Bangladesh Bank Prioritizes Price Stability in Monetary Policy
bd.summary: Bangladesh Bank has maintained a tight monetary policy, raising interest rates to combat persistent inflation that continues to strain household budgets. Economists are debating the effectiveness of these measures as essential commodity prices remain elevated across the country. The central bank emphasizes its commitment to bringing inflation within target levels despite global economic pressures.

international.title: Negotiations for Gaza Ceasefire Intensify Amid Humanitarian Crisis in Rafah
international.summary: International mediators are pushing for renewed negotiations for a ceasefire in Gaza as the humanitarian situation in Rafah deteriorates critically. Israeli military operations continue in the southern Gaza city, leading to widespread displacement and severe shortages of aid, food, and medical supplies. World leaders are urging both Israel and Hamas to agree to a pause in fighting to allow for substantial humanitarian assistance.

international.title: Ukraine Steps Up Drone Attacks on Russian Territory as Western Military Aid Arrives
international.summary: Ukraine has intensified its drone attacks targeting military and energy infrastructure deep inside Russian territory, signaling an escalation in its defensive strategy. These strikes coincide with the increased delivery of advanced Western military aid, including long-range missile systems, which Kyiv says are crucial for defending against ongoing Russian offensives. Both sides continue to report heavy fighting along the eastern front lines.

international.title: French Political Landscape Upended Ahead of Snap Elections, Sending Jitters Across EU Markets
international.summary: France is bracing for snap parliamentary elections after President Macron dissolved the National Assembly, a move that has dramatically reshaped the country's political landscape. Far-right and left-wing blocs are making significant gains in polls, creating uncertainty over the future government and its economic policies. The unexpected election has sent jitters across European financial markets, raising concerns about potential shifts in EU policy direction.
